digest.component_or_area: Gerrit serial auto-lander (project infra: infra/ + project CLI)
propositions: Parallel agent sessions race to land on FFO main, forcing manual rebase-and-retry that burns wall-clock CI minutes and agent attention on non-conflicting changes.

Build a serial auto-lander that, for the front Autosubmit-labeled submittable change/chain, rebases to tip, awaits fresh Verified, and ancestor-atomically submits the exact tested SHA with zero further agent attention.
Expose a single project land command returning one typed terminal outcome (merged | needs_rebase | ci_failed | review_failed) so agents never correlate Gerrit votes/labels/status themselves.
Preserve the strong gate: nothing lands untested (FFO + strict Verified), the two-vote gate is never weakened, and Autosubmit is non-gating, non-sticky, and requester-votable by group Contributors.
Keep all Gerrit-specific landing logic in project infra (infra/ + CLI that may import rebar for ticket ops) with no landing logic added to src/rebar core.
Cross-stack batching is rejected and deferred to bounded speculation (idea c3a9); the platform-agnostic landing provider-seam is deferred to idea 1701.
